# SC-19: Incident Scanning & Detection

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Real-time SIEM monitoring for security incidents using MITRE ATT&CK detection patterns. Classify all detected incidents per DORA Art. 18 taxonomy and trigger the appropriate reporting timelines: 4-hour initial notification, 72-hour intermediate report, 1-month final report for major incidents.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 6 | Observability & Maintenance | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Automated with human escalation |
| **Agent does** | Monitor SIEM continuously; detect and classify incidents; trigger reporting timelines; prepare incident reports. |
| **Human does** | Validate incident classification; approve regulatory reports; make escalation decisions. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Incident detection record | log_entry | Real-time log of all detected security events with DORA Art. 18 classification. | 7 years (DORA) |
| Major incident report | report | Regulatory incident report per DORA Art. 19 timelines (initial, intermediate, final). | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | continuous |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | SIEM platform, MITRE ATT&CK detection rules |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Major incidents trigger immediate escalation and regulatory reporting process.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 17 | ICT-related incident management process including detection and classification |
| DORA | Art. 18 | Classification of ICT-related incidents per DORA taxonomy |
| DORA | Art. 19 | Reporting of major ICT incidents to competent authorities within defined timelines |
| EU AI Act | Art. 73 | Reporting of serious AI incidents to relevant authorities |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
